cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out which of the two options, A or B, is sarcastic. Let me start by understanding the question and the options. The question is asking me to identify the sarcastic statement between the two choices. Sarcasm is a form of irony where someone says the opposite of what they really mean, often with a mocking or scornful tone. So, I need to look at both options and see which one has that tone of insincerity or hidden meaning. Let me look at Option A: "Are you implying history repeats itself if people ignore it? What an absurd claim". The person is responding to someone else's implication that history repeats itself when people ignore it. They're calling the claim "absurd".
